use_case_metadata.title: High-Voltage Hazard: Safe Handling & Recycling of Car Batteries [cite: 2]

target_audience.job_profile: New Manual Laborers / Recycling Line Workers [cite: 2]
target_audience.technical_experience: Low. Likely first time using XR/VR headsets. [cite: 2]
target_audience.accessibility_needs: High contrast for safety labels (color blindness support); Clear audio for loud factory floor simulation. [cite: 2]

learning_objectives: Correctly select and don specific PPE (Acid-resistant gloves, face shield, apron). [cite: 2]
Identify 'Compromised Batteries' (Leaking acid, swollen cases, exposed wiring). [cite: 2]
Execute the 'Terminal Isolation' protocol (Taping nodes to prevent arc flash). [cite: 2]
React instantly to an acid spill or thermal runaway event. [cite: 2]

supporting_elements: 3D Models: Corroded batteries, acid burns (visual representation), proper PPE. [cite: 2]
Audio: Sizzling sounds (acid leak), electric arc buzz (warning sound), urgent alarms. [cite: 2]
Haptic Feedback: Controller vibration when near live terminals. [cite: 2]

scene_description.narrative: The user is placed on a virtual sorting line at a recycling plant. The environment is noisy and industrial. [cite: 2]
scene_description.challenge: Process a pallet of incoming used batteries without causing an explosion, electric shock, or chemical burn. [cite: 2]
scene_description.interaction_type: The scene will be a simple single user demonstration that applies the learning by doing methodology. [cite: 14]

pedagogical_approach.gamification: Minimal. No points for speed. Success is binary: Safe or Unsafe. [cite: 10]
pedagogical_approach.evaluation: Users must complete a 'Perfect Run' (0 safety violations) to pass. Knowledge is tested afterwards with a multiple-choice questionnaire. [cite: 13, 15]

storyboard_script.action: Player spawns into the Clean Room (Locker Area) [cite: 22]
storyboard_script.description: Visuals: The user is in a changing room. A mirror shows the user in street clothes. Audio: Ambient hum of ventilation. [cite: 19, 22]

storyboard_script.action: TERI approaches and briefs the High Stakes [cite: 22]
storyboard_script.description: TERI (MASTER assistant) [cite: 3] runs towards the player: 'Welcome. Before you step onto the floor, understand this: These batteries carry lethal voltage and sulfuric acid. One mistake doesn't just ruin the product; it ruins you. Let's get geared up.' [cite: 22]

Interaction: A table displays various items. TERI: 'Select the gear required for acid protection.' Constraint: Door to the factory will not open until correct PPE (Acid-Resistant Gloves, Face Shield) is equipped. [cite: 3, 20]

storyboard_script.action: Player clicks next / Transition to Factory Floor [cite: 22]
storyboard_script.description: Visuals: Conveyor belts moving batteries. Hazard signs (Skull/Crossbones, High Voltage) are prominent. TERI: 'Approach the sorting table. Treat every battery as if it is leaking and fully charged.' [cite: 19, 22]

Visuals: A battery slides in front of the user with a wet, dark patch. TERI: 'Stop. Look closely. That is sulfuric acid.' Player Action: Must pick up 'Neutralizer Spray' and apply it. Failure Condition: If touched without neutralizing, screen blurs (burn simulation). [cite: 20, 21]
Visuals: A battery with exposed terminals appears. A metal wrench is nearby. TERI: 'The terminals are exposed. Never let conductive material bridge these points.' [cite: 22]

storyboard_script.action: Info Card appears regarding Arc Flash [cite: 5]
storyboard_script.description: Info Card: 'Arc Flash: Temperatures can reach 19,000°F. Always insulate terminals immediately.' [cite: 5, 23]
Player Action: Picks up 'Insulation Tape' and covers the Red (Positive) terminal. Interactive Event: If the wrench moves over the battery before taping, a loud ZAP and flash occur. [cite: 20]

Visuals: A battery on the line bursts (scripted event). TERI[cite: 3]: 'Containment breach! Acid spray! Find the safety shower!' Player Action: Must physically turn and pull the Safety Shower lever within 5 seconds. [cite: 20]
TERI: 'You reacted correctly to the emergency. You have isolated the terminals and inspected for leaks. You are ready for the floor.' Action: A digital 'Safety Certificate' appears. [cite: 23]
